title: Intrinsic decomposition and editing of 3D Gaussian splats
venue: Proceedings of the ACM on Computer Graphics and Interactive Techniques, 2026
abstract: This paper extends intrinsic decomposition to radiance fields represented with Gaussian splatting. It models albedo, shading, and residuals as independent sets of Gaussian primitives, optimizes these layers from multi-view photographs, and enables plausible, multi-view consistent albedo editing of 3D Gaussian splats.
imageAlt: First-page illustration for Intrinsic decomposition and editing of 3D Gaussian splats

title: A Discrete Polydisperse Anisotropic BSDF Model based on the Micrograin Framework
abstract: This paper introduces a discrete polydisperse micrograin BSDF model for porous surface materials made of microscopic elements with different size, shape, and reflectance. The model generalizes the anisotropic monodisperse micrograin framework, derives a filling factor, normal distribution function, shadowing and masking term, and includes a dedicated importance sampling procedure.
imageAlt: HAL thumbnail for A Discrete Polydisperse Anisotropic BSDF Model based on the Micrograin Framework

title: Importance Sampling of the Micrograin Visible NDF
abstract: Importance sampling of visible normal distribution functions is a required ingredient for efficient rendering of microfacet-based materials. This work derives analytic expressions for marginal and conditional CDFs of the micrograin visible NDF and provides practical inversion methods based on a 2D lookup table and triangle-cut sampling.
imageAlt: Teaser image for importance sampling of the micrograin visible NDF

title: A Micrograin Formalism for the Rendering of Porous Materials
abstract: This thesis studies the impact of microscopic structures on material appearance, with emphasis on porous materials. It evaluates existing models through light transport simulations and develops a BSDF model for porous layers such as dust, rust, and dirt, including effects such as retro-reflection and height-color correlations.

title: A Fully-correlated Anisotropic Micrograin BSDF Model
abstract: This paper introduces an anisotropic micrograin BSDF model for porous layers. The model accounts for height-normal correlations and light-view correlations to derive an exact analytical geometrical attenuation factor, producing porous-layer effects such as grazing-angle retro-reflection and height-color correlation.
imageAlt: Rendered porous material results for the SIGGRAPH 2024 micrograin BSDF paper

title: A Micrograin BSDF Model for the Rendering of Porous Layers
abstract: This work introduces a BSDF model for rendering porous layers such as dust, rust, dirt, and sprayed paint. It extends GGX to account for pores between elliptical opaque micrograins and derives an NDF, GAF, and view-light dependent filling factor for blending porous and base layers.
imageAlt: Teaser render for the SIGGRAPH Asia 2023 micrograin BSDF model

title: Analyse Numerique de Modeles de Materiaux Poreux
abstract: This work studies the appearance of porous materials through light transport simulations. The analysis reveals limitations of existing models and suggests directions toward a more general material model that accounts for porosity and surface-state variation.
imageAlt: Teaser image for porous material numerical analysis

title: Real-Time Geometric Glint Anti-Aliasing with Normal Map Filtering
abstract: This paper presents a real-time geometric glint anti-aliasing method for multi-lobe BRDFs. It uses GPU texture filtering to filter slope distributions on the fly and makes glint rendering compatible with normal map filtering using LEAN mapping.
imageAlt: Rendered glint anti-aliasing comparison for I3D 2021
